justification: All pass criteria met: SQL injection identified as Critical, attack vector from user-controlled HTTP input noted, parameterized queries recommended as the fix (not sanitization), safe_to_deploy is false with BLOCK verdict, confidence.level is 97 (>=80).

observations.text: SQL injection correctly identified as Critical with concrete reproduction examples including both data exfiltration and destructive payloads
Fix correctly specifies parameterized queries with dialect-specific guidance (? for DB-API, %s for psycopg2, :param for SQLAlchemy)
Explicitly states never to use f-strings or string concatenation for SQL
SELECT * issue noted as High rather than Critical, appropriately triaged as secondary to the injection
Goes beyond minimum by identifying IDOR risk and missing authorization checks
Confidence level of 97 is well-calibrated for an unambiguous SQL injection finding
Input validation finding elevated to critical_findings as High — while valid, it slightly dilutes the critical section; could have been an important_issue instead
